chapter_title.ar: باب كَرَاهِيَةِ أَنْ يَبِيتَ الْإِنْسَانُ وَحْدَهُ وَ الْخِصَالِ الْمَنْهِيِّ عَنْهَا لِعِلَّةٍ مَخُوفَةٍ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 69 – Abhorrence for the human being to sleep alone, and the characteristics Prohibited about it for the reason of fearfulness

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/449)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/449)
text: لِمُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سُلَيْمَانَ أَيْنَ نَزَلْتَ قَالَ فِي مَكَانِ كَذَا وَ كَذَا قَالَ مَعَكَ أَحَدٌ قَالَ لَا قَالَ لَا تَكُنْ وَحْدَكَ تَحَوَّلْ عَنْهُ يَا مَيْمُونُ فَإِنَّ الشَّيْطَانَ أَجْرَأَ مَا يَكُونُ عَلَى الْإِنْسَانِ إِذَا كَانَ وَحْدَهُ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ja’far Bin Muhammad Al Ashary, from Ibn Al Qadah, from his father Maymoun,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said to Muhammad Bin Suleyman: ‘Where have you lodged?’ He said, ‘In such a such place’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Is there anyone with you?’ He said, ‘No’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Do not be alone, transfer from it, O Maymoun, for the Satan<sup>la</sup> would be the most audacious upon the human being when he was alone’.
en.sarwar: 7. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Ja‘far ibn Muhammad al-Ash‘ariy from ibn al-Qaddah from his father Maymun who has said the following: “Abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, once asked Muhammad ibn Sulayman, ‘Where have you found lodging?’ He replied, ‘It is in so and so place.’ He (the Imam) asked, ‘Is there anyone else with you?’ he replied, ‘No, there is no one with me.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘Move from there, O Maymun and do not stay there alone; Satan is most daring against man when he is alone.’”
